items.title: Why are Afghan migrants and refugees being forced to flee Iran? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>So far this year more than 1.5 million Afghan refugees and migrants have left Iran under duress to go home, according to the UN's International Organization for Migration.  Aid groups warn that more than a million others may be forced to return by the end of the year. Many of the returnees say an already strained atmosphere turned hostile in June after Iran and Israel fought a brief war against each other. 

00:00 Afghans forced to leave Iran
03:33 Dr. Khadija Abbasi, Teaching Fellow in the Department of Development Studies at the School of Oriental and African Studies in London talks to DW

#afghanistan #iran #migration

items.title: How Belgium became a hotspot for cocaine trafficking | Focus on Europe
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Belgium is a cocaine hub in Europe. The drug floods the continent through the port of Antwerp. Violence is also escalating in some districts of Brussels. Shootings and drug deals in plain sight are everyday occurrences for some people there.

items.title: Will Trump envoy Witkoff's visit to Gaza make a difference? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Donald Trump's special envoy, Steve Witkoff, has said he is working on a new Gaza aid plan after inspecting a controversial food distribution center on Friday. 
The UN says hundreds of people have been killed in the vicinity of the Israeli and US-backed distribution facilities, though Israel maintains that its forces do not intentionally target civilians. More than 60,000 people are reported to have died in the conflict in Gaza, with international anger growing at the deepening starvation crisis.

Chapters
00:00 Trump special envoy Witkoff promises more aid for Gaza
02:34 DW speaks with Nomi Bar-Yaacov, one of the leading international human rights lawyers and negotiators and a fellow at the Geneva Centre for Security Policy

items.title: India’s threat to dry out Pakistan | Mapped Out
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>India has threatened to stop the flow of the Indus River, which flows on to Pakistan, following recent fighting in and around the disputed Kashmir region. The threat caused panic and anger in Pakistan, which is highly dependent on this water for its drinking supply and agriculture. For decades, a unique treaty ensured.

Chapters
00:00 The Indus River: A lifeline for millions
01:03 Indus water war
06:32 The history of Indus Waters Treaty
09:15 Indus and the conflict in Kashmir
13:05 The future of the Indus River

#MappedOut #IndusRiver #India #Pakistan

items.author: DW News

items.title: Trump hits Brazil with tariffs over Bolsonaro trial | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Brazil’s finance minister says the Trump administration’s tariffs are “more favourable” than he expected, but that there is still “a lot of injustice” in the measures. Trump's order adds a 40 percent levy on Brazilian goods, bringing the total up to 50 percent. The tariffs affect coffee and meat, two major Brazilian exports, but exempts hundreds of other products. Today in Sao Paulo, demonstrators gathered outside the US Consulate. They claim that Trump is using tariffs to force Brazil into dropping the trial of his ally, former Brazilian president Jair Bolsonaro. 

00:00 Over half of Brazilian exports have 50% tariff from August 6
00:42 Welber Barral, International trade specialist
04:45 Thiago de Aragao, Political analyst


For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Trump's ever-shifting tariff policy stoking uncertainty | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>August 1 was supposed to be a make or break day for country's doing business with the United States.  US President Trump's offer was clear:  either negotiate a new trade deal with his team or face higher tariffs. Yesterday some key US trade partners such as Canada, India and Brazil were still without a new deal. Just hours before the deadline, the US President pushed it into the future.

00:00 Trump's ever-shifting tariff policy stoking uncertainty
02:05 Clayton Allen, Eurasia Group
13:22 Tyson Barker, Atlantic Council
19:05 Steven Beardsley, DW Business

items.title: Trump says he sent two nuclear subs toward Russia | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump says he has ordered two nuclear submarines to be positioned to 'appropriate regions' closer to Russia. It comes as, what he says is a response to "highly provocative" comments made by Russia's former president and current Deputy Chairman of the Security Council Dmitry Medvedev. Trump did not specify what exactly Medvedev had said to antagonize him.

items.title: Arab states back call for Hamas to disarm | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The dire humanitarian situation in Gaza prompted several western countries to announce this week that they would recognize a Palestinian state. Efforts to breathe new life into a two-state solution were also backed by the Arab League's members this week. They unanimously endorsed a declaration calling for Hamas to lay down its weapons, release all hostages and end its rule of Gaza. The call for Hamas to disarm came at a United Nations conference on a two-state solution to end the decades-long conflict between Israel and the Palestinians. The meeting aimed to build political momentum towards formal recognition of Palestinian statehood. Israel and the US refused to attend, saying discussing a two-state solution now would only embolden Hamas. 

00:00 Arab states back call for Hamas to disarm 
01:01 Karim El-Gawhary, Journalist


For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Why injecting rhino horns with radioactive isotopes protects against poaching | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Under a joint project in South Africa, rhino horns are being injected with radioactive isotopes. The method does not harm the animals and allows detection by border officials. Tests showed horns can be found inside sealed 40-foot containers. About 20 rhinos were treated in initial trials last year.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: German foreign minister visits Israel to push for change in Gaza | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Germany's foreign minister has warned Israel not to annexe any Palestinian territory in Gaza or the occupied West Bank. Johann Wadephul urged Israel to allow more aid into Gaza during talks with senior politicians from both sides. He also condemned violence committed against Palestinians by Israeli settlers.

items.title: Germany condemns settler violence as “terror” | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Germany’s foreign minister used a two-day diplomatic trip to the Middle East to condemn Israeli settler violence as “terror.”  At time of publication, Israel had not responded to his remarks. Wadephul was in the West Bank to meet Palestinian Authority leaders for talks.
